description_long: This research institute building features a rectangular, elongated structure with a flat roof and multiple evenly spaced windows along its facades. The exterior walls are finished in a light gray color, complemented by a darker roof and trim. The building is divided internally into several rooms or laboratories, separated by partition walls, and accessed via a central corridor. The main entrance is marked by a prominent glass double-door section at the front, flanked by large windows that provide natural light to the lobby area. The overall design is functional and institutional, with minimal decorative elements, focusing on practicality and efficient use of space. The structure appears to be constructed primarily from concrete and glass, typical of modern educational or research facilities.
description: A rectangular, flat-roofed research institute with light gray walls, dark trim, and many windows, featuring a central glass entryway and multiple internal rooms.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main glass entryway, large windows, and the symmetrical arrangement of rooms inside.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the building, the central corridor, and the arrangement of internal rooms.
description_view_2: Rear view with evenly spaced windows and no visible entryway, highlighting the building's length.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the corridor and room layout from the other end.
attribute_entryway_design: The main entryway is centrally located on the front facade, featuring a wide glass double-door framed by dark trim. The entry is flush with the ground, with no visible steps, suggesting accessible entry. Large glass panels on either side of the doors provide additional light and visibility into the lobby. The design is simple and functional, with no decorative elements.
